title: ABC: A Big CAD Model Dataset For Geometric Deep Learning
abstract: We introduce ABC-Dataset, a collection of one million Computer-Aided Design (CAD) models for research of geometric deep learning methods and applications. Each model is a collection of explicitly parametrized curves and surfaces, providing ground truth for differential quantities, patch segmentation, geometric feature detection, and shape reconstruction.
method_summary: Introduces ABC dataset: 1M CAD models with explicit parametric curves/surfaces. Provides ground truth for normals, curvature, segmentation, feature detection, and reconstruction. Models sourced from Onshape, stored as STEP files. Enables benchmarking of geometric deep learning on real engineering shapes.

title: DeepCAD: A Deep Generative Network for Computer-Aided Design Models
abstract: We present a deep generative network for Computer-Aided Design (CAD) models that can generate both the 3D shape and the construction sequence. To train our network, we create a new CAD dataset consisting of 178,238 models and their CAD construction sequences.
method_summary: Introduces DeepCAD dataset: 178K CAD models with construction sequences (sketch-and-extrude operations). Each model represented as a sequence of parametric commands. Extracted from ABC/Onshape. Became the de facto standard benchmark for parametric CAD generation, widely used for evaluating autoencoding and generation quality.

title: Fusion 360 Gallery: A Dataset and Environment for Programmatic CAD Construction from Human Design Sequences
abstract: We present the Fusion 360 Gallery reconstruction dataset, containing 8,625 human designed CAD sequences suitable for use with machine learning. The dataset enables learning-based approaches using sequential sketch and extrude modeling operations.
method_summary: Introduces Fusion 360 Gallery: 8,625 human-authored CAD design sequences from Autodesk Fusion 360. Includes B-rep models, construction sequences, assembly data, and segmentation annotations. Also introduces Fusion 360 Gym, an interactive RL environment that formulates CAD construction as an MDP. Unique in capturing real human design intent.

title: SketchGraphs: A Large-Scale Dataset for Modeling Relational Geometry in Computer-Aided Design
abstract: To facilitate this research, we introduce SketchGraphs, a collection of 15 million sketches extracted from real-world CAD models coupled with an open-source data processing pipeline. Each sketch is represented as a geometric constraint graph.
method_summary: Introduces SketchGraphs: 15M 2D parametric sketches from real-world Onshape CAD models. Each sketch represented as a constraint graph with geometric primitives (lines, arcs, circles) and explicit constraints (perpendicular, tangent, coincident). Largest CAD sketch dataset. Enables constraint inference and auto-completion research.

title: Zero-to-CAD: Agentic Synthesis of Interpretable CAD Programs at Million-Scale Without Real Data
abstract: We frame synthesis as an agentic search problem: by embedding a large language model within a feedback-driven CAD environment, our system iteratively generates, executes, and validates code using compiler and geometric feedback. This agentic approach enables the synthesis of approximately one million executable, readable, editable CAD sequences.
method_summary: Introduces Zero-to-CAD dataset: ~1M synthetically generated CAD programs via agentic LLM-driven synthesis. No real training data needed. Uses CadQuery programs with compiler+geometric feedback loop. Covers operations beyond sketch-and-extrude (lofts, sweeps, fillets). Demonstrates agentic data synthesis paradigm for CAD. Released as Zero-To-CAD-1m on HuggingFace.

title: A Large-Scale Annotated Mechanical Components Benchmark for Classification and Retrieval Tasks with Deep Neural Networks
abstract: We introduce a large-scale annotated mechanical components benchmark for classification and retrieval tasks named Mechanical Components Benchmark (MCB): a large-scale dataset of 3D objects of mechanical components.
method_summary: Introduces MCB: large-scale benchmark of 3D mechanical components from TraceParts (68 categories, 58K+ models). Annotated for classification and retrieval. Evaluates shape descriptors and deep learning methods on engineering-specific shapes. Addresses gap between generic 3D shape benchmarks and real mechanical engineering needs.

title: MFCAD: A Dataset of 3D CAD Models with Machining Feature Labels
abstract: A dataset of 3D CAD models with machining feature labels for training and evaluating machining feature recognition methods using deep learning.
method_summary: Introduces MFCAD dataset: 3D B-rep CAD models annotated with machining feature labels (holes, slots, pockets, bosses, etc.). Designed for training deep learning-based machining feature recognition. Extended by MFCAD++ with more complex intersecting features. Key benchmark for manufacturing-oriented CAD understanding.

title: FloorPlanCAD: A Large-Scale CAD Drawing Dataset for Panoptic Symbol Spotting
abstract: We present FloorPlanCAD, a large-scale real-world CAD drawing dataset containing over 15,000 floor plans, ranging from residential to commercial buildings. CAD drawings in the dataset are annotated with line-grained vector graphics.
method_summary: Introduces FloorPlanCAD: 15K+ real-world architectural floor plan CAD drawings with line-level vector annotations. Covers residential, commercial, school, hospital buildings. Defines panoptic symbol spotting task for 2D CAD. Provides train/val/test splits and evaluation metrics for symbol detection and segmentation.

title: An Open Large-Scale Architectural CAD Dataset and New Baseline for Panoptic Symbol Spotting
abstract: We construct ArchCAD-400K, a large-scale CAD dataset consisting of 413,062 chunks from 5,538 highly standardized drawings, making it over 26 times larger than the largest existing CAD dataset. ArchCAD-400K boasts extended drawing diversity and broader categories with line-grained annotations.
method_summary: Introduces ArchCAD-400K: 413K annotated chunks from 5,538 architectural drawings. 26x larger than FloorPlanCAD. 5 aligned modalities (vector, raster, text, spatial, semantic). Built via automated CAD parsing engine. Proposes Dual-Path Spotting Network baseline. Addresses scale limitation of prior 2D CAD datasets.

title: SESYD: A Synthetic Document Database for Performance Evaluation
abstract: The SESYD database is composed of eleven collections for performance evaluation containing 284K images, 190K symbols and 284K characters for document image analysis.
method_summary: Introduces SESYD: synthetic engineering drawing database with 284K images, 190K symbols, 284K characters across 11 collections. Includes floor plans, electrical diagrams, and architectural drawings. Synthetic generation enables controlled evaluation. Long-standing benchmark for symbol recognition in technical drawings.

title: VideoCAD: A Large-Scale Video Dataset for Learning UI Interactions and 3D Reasoning from CAD Software
abstract: VideoCAD is a large-scale synthetic dataset consisting of over 41K annotated video recordings of CAD operations, generated using an automated framework for collecting high-fidelity UI action data from the Onshape CAD platform.
method_summary: Introduces VideoCAD: 41K+ annotated video recordings of CAD UI interactions in Onshape. Captures mouse clicks, keystrokes, cursor movements, and 3D state changes. Enables learning long-horizon UI interaction policies and VQA tasks. First dataset bridging video understanding and CAD operation learning. Includes CADAgent transformer model.

title: CAD-MLLM: Unifying Multimodality-Conditioned CAD Generation With MLLM
abstract: Our resulting dataset, named Omni-CAD, is the first multimodal CAD dataset that contains textual description, multi-view images, points, and command sequence for each CAD model. It contains approximately 450K instances.
method_summary: Introduces Omni-CAD dataset: ~450K CAD models with 4 aligned modalities (text descriptions, multi-view images, point clouds, parametric command sequences). Built on DeepCAD data with automated annotation pipeline. First comprehensive multimodal CAD dataset. Enables text/image/point-cloud conditioned CAD generation.

title: SldprtNet: A Large-Scale Multimodal Dataset for CAD Generation in Language-Driven 3D Design
abstract: We introduce SldprtNet, a large-scale dataset comprising over 242,000 industrial parts, designed for semantic-driven CAD modeling, geometric deep learning, and the training/fine-tuning of multimodal models.
method_summary: Introduces SldprtNet: 242K+ industrial CAD parts from SolidWorks with multimodal annotations (3D geometry, text descriptions, parametric features). Sourced from real industrial designs. Supports language-driven CAD generation and geometric deep learning. Larger and more industrially representative than DeepCAD.

title: A Cascade MAR with Topology Predictor for Multimodal Conditional CAD Generation
abstract: To facilitate large-scale training, we develop a large-scale multimodal CAD dataset, mmABC, which includes over 1.3 million B-Rep models with multimodal annotations, including point clouds, text descriptions, and multi-view images.
method_summary: Introduces mmABC dataset: 1.3M+ B-Rep CAD models with multimodal annotations (point clouds, text, multi-view images). Built from ABC dataset with automated annotation. Largest multimodal B-Rep dataset. Supports multimodal conditional B-Rep generation with topology prediction.

title: mrCAD: Multimodal Refinement of Computer-aided Designs
abstract: We present mrCAD, a dataset of multimodal instructions in a communication game where one player describes edits to a CAD model and another player executes them, bridging the gap between how humans and machines perform edits.
method_summary: Introduces mrCAD dataset: multimodal CAD editing instructions from a communication game. Captures natural language + visual descriptions of design edits. Tests whether models can follow iterative refinement instructions. Unique focus on human-machine interaction for CAD editing rather than generation from scratch.

title: GenCAD-3D: CAD Program Generation using Multimodal Latent Space Alignment and Synthetic Dataset Balancing
abstract: We introduce SynthBal, a synthetic data augmentation strategy specifically designed to balance and expand datasets, notably enhancing representation of complex CAD geometries. Our experiments show that SynthBal significantly boosts reconstruction accuracy and reduces generation of invalid CAD models.
method_summary: Proposes SynthBal: synthetic data augmentation for CAD datasets that balances complexity distribution. Addresses the long-tail problem where simple shapes dominate training data. Generates synthetic complex CAD programs to augment underrepresented categories. Demonstrates significant improvement on high-complexity geometries.

title: CADEvolve: Creating Realistic CAD via Program Evolution
abstract: We present CADEvolve, an evolution-based pipeline and dataset that starts from simple primitives and, via VLM-guided edits and validations, incrementally grows CAD programs toward industrial-grade complexity.
method_summary: Introduces CADEvolve: evolution-based synthetic CAD data generation pipeline. Uses VLM-guided mutations to evolve simple CAD primitives into complex industrial shapes. Creates dataset of progressively complex CadQuery programs. Demonstrates that evolved synthetic data improves Image2CAD performance. Alternative agentic synthesis approach to Zero-to-CAD.

title: Learning From Design Procedure To Generate CAD Programs for Data Augmentation
abstract: We propose a novel data augmentation paradigm that prompts an LLM to generate CAD programs conditioned on a reference surface program and a modeling procedure, inspired by professional CAD design workflows.
method_summary: Proposes procedure-conditioned CAD data augmentation: uses LLM to generate new CAD programs by following design procedures extracted from reference models. Addresses limited geometric complexity in LLM-generated CAD. Augments training data with more diverse and complex shapes. Demonstrates improved generation quality on downstream tasks.

title: Quantitative Evaluation of 3D Models Generation by Large Language Models
abstract: We propose a comprehensive suite of similarity and complexity metrics including volumetric accuracy, surface alignment, dimensional fidelity, and topological intricacy to benchmark generated models against reference designs.
method_summary: Proposes comprehensive evaluation framework for LLM-generated 3D/CAD models. Metrics include: volumetric accuracy (IoU), surface alignment (Chamfer Distance), dimensional fidelity, and topological intricacy. Human-in-the-loop evaluation protocol. Benchmarks multiple LLMs on CAD code generation. Addresses lack of standardized evaluation for text-to-3D/CAD.

title: CAD-Judge: Toward Efficient Morphological Grading and Verification for Text-to-CAD Generation
abstract: Text-to-CAD systems aim to generate editable, general-purpose CAD models from textual descriptions. We propose CAD-Judge with a Compiler-as-a-Judge Module and prospect theory to efficiently grade and validate generated CAD models.
method_summary: Proposes CAD-Judge: automated evaluation framework for text-to-CAD generation. Uses Compiler-as-a-Judge module to verify compilability and geometric validity. Applies prospect theory for morphological grading. Evaluates both structural correctness (compilation success) and geometric fidelity. First dedicated evaluation tool for text-to-CAD systems.

title: Generating CAD Code with Vision-Language Models for 3D Designs
abstract: Testing the correctness of CAD-generated code is challenging due to the structural intricacies of 3D objects. We introduce CADCodeVerify, a novel approach for verifying CAD code correctness through visual self-verification.
method_summary: Introduces CADCodeVerify: visual self-verification framework for CAD code generation. VLMs generate CAD code then verify output by comparing rendered views against specifications. Proposes compilation success rate and visual consistency as evaluation metrics. Demonstrates that visual feedback loop improves CAD generation quality. Addresses the evaluation gap for programmatic CAD.

title: Geometrically Constrained Parametric History-based CAD Dataset
abstract: Parametric computer-aided design (CAD) modeling is fundamental to industrial design, but existing datasets often lack explicit geometric constraints and fine-grained functional semantics, limiting editability evaluation.
method_summary: Introduces a CAD dataset with explicit geometric constraints and functional semantics. Addresses limitation of existing datasets that lack constraint information needed to evaluate editability. Includes parametric history with constraint graphs. Enables evaluation of constraint satisfaction and design intent preservation in generated CAD models.

title: Text2CAD: Generating Sequential CAD Models from Beginner-to-Expert Level Text Prompts
abstract: We propose Text2CAD, the first AI framework for generating text-to-parametric CAD models using designer-friendly instructions for all skill levels. We introduce a data annotation pipeline for generating text prompts at different expertise levels.
method_summary: Introduces text annotation pipeline for DeepCAD dataset: generates beginner/intermediate/expert level text descriptions for 170K+ CAD models. Creates first large-scale text-CAD paired dataset. Proposes evaluation metrics for text-to-CAD including compilation rate, geometric similarity (CD, F-score), and text-shape alignment. NeurIPS 2024 Spotlight.

title: BlenderLLM: Training Large Language Models for Computer-Aided Design with Self-improvement
abstract: We present BlenderLLM, a novel framework for training LLMs specifically for CAD tasks leveraging a self-improvement methodology. We developed a bespoke training dataset and evaluation benchmark for CAD script generation.
method_summary: Introduces CAD-specific training dataset and evaluation benchmark for LLM-based CAD generation. Evaluates compilation success rate, geometric accuracy, and script quality. Reveals significant limitations of existing LLMs in CAD script generation. Proposes self-improvement training loop with iterative refinement. Provides baseline benchmark results across multiple LLMs.

title: Reinforcement Learning Training Gym for Revolution Involved CAD Command Sequence Generation
abstract: We present a reinforcement learning training gym specifically designed for CAD model generation, along with an RL-based algorithm that generates command sequences from boundary representations including revolution operations.
method_summary: Introduces RL training gym for CAD generation extending beyond extrusion to revolution operations. Provides interactive environment with reward signals based on geometric similarity. Extends Fusion 360 Gym concept to broader operation vocabulary. Addresses evaluation of CAD generation with non-standard operations.

title: DeepPatent2: A Large-Scale Benchmarking Corpus for Technical Drawing Understanding
abstract: We introduce DeepPatent2, a large-scale dataset providing more than 2.7 million technical drawings with 132,890 object names and 22,394 viewpoints extracted from 14 years of US design patent documents.
method_summary: Introduces DeepPatent2: 2.7M technical/patent drawings from USPTO spanning 14 years. Annotated with object names (132K) and viewpoints (22K). Supports image retrieval, captioning, and cross-modal analysis of engineering drawings. Largest technical drawing dataset. Bridges gap between CAD and document understanding.

title: Enginuity: Building an Open Multi-Domain Dataset of Complex Engineering Diagrams
abstract: We propose Enginuity, the first open, large-scale, multi-domain engineering diagram dataset with comprehensive structural annotations designed for automated diagram parsing.
method_summary: Introduces Enginuity: first open multi-domain engineering diagram dataset with structural annotations. Covers process flow diagrams, P&IDs, electrical schematics, and architectural drawings. Designed for automated diagram parsing. Provides entity and relationship annotations. Addresses lack of open engineering diagram benchmarks.

title: Slice-100K: A Multimodal Dataset for Extrusion-based 3D Printing
abstract: We present Slice-100K, a first-of-its-kind dataset of over 100,000 G-code files, along with their tessellated CAD model, LVIS categories, and renderings for extrusion-based additive manufacturing.
method_summary: Introduces Slice-100K: 100K+ paired STL-to-G-code files for 3D printing. Multimodal: CAD meshes, G-code toolpaths, LVIS categories, rendered images. Bridges CAD design and manufacturing. Enables research on CAD-to-manufacturing pipeline. First large-scale dataset connecting CAD models to fabrication instructions.

title: Geometric Deep Learning for Computer-Aided Design: A Survey
abstract: By harnessing the power of machine learning-based methods, CAD designers can optimize their workflows. This survey covers geometric deep learning methods for CAD including datasets, representations, and evaluation approaches.
method_summary: Comprehensive survey of geometric deep learning for CAD. Catalogs major CAD datasets (ABC, DeepCAD, Fusion360, SketchGraphs) and their characteristics. Reviews evaluation metrics used across the field (CD, EMD, F-score, validity, novelty). Discusses data representation challenges and benchmark gaps. Key reference for understanding CAD dataset landscape.

title: CAD Arena: Open Benchmark for AI-Generated Parametric CAD
abstract: CAD Arena is an open benchmark platform that compares outputs from LLM baselines, academic, and commercial text-to-CAD models side by side on a fixed set of 20 curated prompts across 4 difficulty tiers.
method_summary: Introduces CAD Arena: open online benchmark for text-to-CAD evaluation. 20 curated prompts across 4 difficulty tiers. Side-by-side comparison of LLM baselines, academic models, and commercial systems. Elo-style ranking. Addresses lack of standardized text-to-CAD benchmarks. Community-driven evaluation platform.

title: CadVLM: Bridging Language and Vision in the Generation of Parametric CAD Sketches
abstract: Parametric Computer-Aided Design (CAD) is central to contemporary mechanical design. However, it encounters challenges in achieving precise parametric sketch modeling and lacks practical evaluation metrics.
method_summary: Proposes CadVLM with new evaluation metrics for parametric CAD sketch generation. Introduces sketch-specific metrics beyond standard CD/EMD: constraint satisfaction rate, primitive accuracy, and parametric precision. Highlights the gap between geometric similarity metrics and actual CAD quality (editability, constraint correctness).

title: Aligning Constraint Generation with Design Intent in Parametric CAD
abstract: A critical first step towards aligning generative CAD models is to generate constraints which fully-constrain all geometric primitives, without over-constraining or distorting sketch geometry.
method_summary: Proposes evaluation framework for constraint generation in parametric CAD. Metrics: full-constraint rate, over-constraint rate, geometry distortion. Evaluates whether generated constraints preserve design intent. Addresses the rarely-measured but critical aspect of CAD quality: constraint correctness and editability.

title: State Space Model For 3D Computer-Aided Design Generative Modeling
abstract: Recently, the parametric CAD sequence as the design logic of an object has been widely mined by sequence models. However, industrial CAD models are fine-grained and complex, requiring long sequences. We introduce DeepCAD-240 benchmark for long-form CAD generation.
method_summary: Introduces DeepCAD-240: benchmark for long-form parametric CAD sequence generation with up to 240 sketch-extrusion operations. Mined from ABC dataset with complexity filtering. Addresses limitation of DeepCAD (max ~60 operations) for evaluating generation of complex industrial parts. Tests scalability of generative models to realistic sequence lengths.

title: Large Language Models for Computer-Aided Design: A Survey
abstract: Comprehensive survey examining the intersection of LLMs and CAD systems. Reviews both proprietary and open-source models, presents a taxonomy of six key application areas where LLMs impact CAD, and proposes future research directions for continued innovation.
method_summary: Survey paper covering LLM foundations for CAD, application taxonomy across six areas, and analysis of both commercial and open-source tool ecosystems for LLM-CAD integration.

title: CAD-Assistant: Tool-Augmented VLLMs as Generic CAD Task Solvers
abstract: Proposes a general-purpose CAD agent leveraging a Vision and Large Language Model as a planner combined with tool-augmentation using CAD-specific tools. The system processes multimodal user queries by generating actions executed on FreeCAD via its Python API, including specialized tools like a sketch image parameterizer, rendering modules, and a 2D cross-section generator.
method_summary: VLLM-based CAD agent with tool augmentation paradigm: uses FreeCAD Python API as execution backend, specialized CAD tools for sketch parameterization and rendering, and adaptive action generation based on evolving design state.

title: From Idea to CAD: A Language Model-Driven Multi-Agent System for Collaborative Design
abstract: Describes a system that automates CAD model creation by mirroring a typical engineering team structure. Uses vision language models and multiple specialized agents—one for requirements, one for CAD work, and one for quality checks—to generate designs from sketches or text descriptions with iterative refinement.
method_summary: Multi-agent system with VLM-based specialized agents (requirements, CAD modeling, quality checking) that mirrors engineering team structure for collaborative parametric CAD design from text/sketch inputs.

title: Agent-Aided Design for Dynamic CAD Models
abstract: Introduces AADvark, a system that addresses the inability of existing agentic CAD systems to create complex 3D assemblies with moving parts. Captures dynamic part interactions with degrees-of-freedom, integrating constraint solver tools and specialized visual feedback to enable construction of functional 3D models with moving components.
method_summary: AADvark: agentic CAD system for dynamic assemblies with DOF reasoning, constraint solver integration, and visual feedback loops for movable 3D model construction.

title: Text-to-CadQuery: A New Paradigm for CAD Generation with Scalable Large Model Capabilities
abstract: Proposes generating CadQuery code directly from text, leveraging pretrained LLMs to produce 3D models without intermediate representations. Augmented Text2CAD dataset with 170K CadQuery annotations and fine-tuned six open-source LLMs, achieving 69.3% top-1 exact match and 48.6% Chamfer Distance reduction.
method_summary: Direct text-to-CadQuery code generation using fine-tuned open-source LLMs on 170K augmented annotations, bypassing intermediate CAD representations for scalable parametric model generation.

title: CAD-Coder: Text-to-CAD Generation with Chain-of-Thought and Geometric Reward
abstract: Reformulates text-to-CAD as generation of CadQuery scripts. Combines supervised fine-tuning with reinforcement learning using geometric rewards based on Chamfer Distance, incorporates chain-of-thought planning, and leverages a dataset of 110K text-CadQuery-3D model triplets to generate diverse, valid, and complex CAD models from natural language.
method_summary: Two-stage pipeline (SFT + RL with geometric reward) for CadQuery script generation from text, using chain-of-thought planning and 110K training triplets.

title: CAD-Coder: An Open-Source Vision-Language Model for Computer-Aided Design Code Generation
abstract: Fine-tuned vision-language model for generating editable CAD code in CadQuery Python from visual inputs. Built on GenCAD-Code dataset of 163K+ image-code pairs, outperforms GPT-4.5 and Qwen2.5-VL-72B with 100% valid syntax rate. Demonstrates generalization on real-world images and unseen CAD operations.
method_summary: Open-source VLM fine-tuned on 163K image-CadQuery pairs achieving 100% syntax validity, demonstrating that specialized open models can surpass proprietary VLMs for CAD code generation.

title: TOOLCAD: Exploring Tool-Using Large Language Models in Text-to-CAD Generation with Reinforcement Learning
abstract: Deploys LLMs as tool-using agents for text-to-CAD generation. Introduces an interactive CAD modeling environment and end-to-end post-training strategy using online curriculum reinforcement learning. Demonstrates that open-source LLMs for CAD tool-using agents can achieve performance comparable to proprietary models.
method_summary: Tool-augmented LLM agent for CAD with interactive modeling environment and curriculum RL training, enabling open-source models to match proprietary model performance in text-to-CAD.

title: CADDesigner: Conceptual Design of CAD Models Based on General-Purpose Agent
abstract: LLM-powered agent that streamlines CAD design by accepting text and sketches as input. Through interactive dialogue, analyzes requirements and generates modeling code using a novel Explicit Context Imperative Paradigm. Incorporates iterative visual feedback and maintains a knowledge base for continuous improvement.
method_summary: General-purpose LLM agent with Context-Independent Imperative Paradigm for CAD code generation, iterative visual feedback, and persistent knowledge base for design improvement.

title: Generative AI for CAD Automation: Leveraging Large Language Models for 3D Modelling
abstract: Explores using LLMs to streamline CAD workflows by integrating them with FreeCAD. Generates initial design scripts from plain language, then refines iteratively based on execution feedback. Testing shows LLMs handle simple-to-moderate complexity well but struggle with highly constrained models, identifying gaps in memory systems, prompting strategies, and hybrid AI approaches.
method_summary: LLM-FreeCAD integration framework with iterative script refinement from natural language, identifying capability boundaries and improvement directions for LLM-driven CAD automation.

title: Clarify Before You Draw: Proactive Agents for Robust Text-to-CAD Generation
abstract: Proposes ProCAD, a framework that resolves specification issues before code synthesis. Uses a clarifying agent to audit prompts and pose targeted questions, paired with a coding agent that generates CadQuery programs. Achieves 79.9% reduction in geometric error and lowers invalid outputs from 4.8% to 0.9%.
method_summary: Proactive dual-agent system (clarifying agent + coding agent) for robust text-to-CAD via CadQuery, resolving ambiguity before synthesis rather than reactively fixing errors.

title: Query2CAD: Generating CAD models using natural language queries
abstract: Framework using GPT-4 Turbo to generate executable CAD macros with self-refinement capabilities. Achieves 53.6% success rate on initial attempts, improving to 76.7% after refinement iterations. Incorporates BLIP2 model feedback and human-in-the-loop validation without supervised training data.
method_summary: Zero-shot LLM-based CAD macro generation with self-refinement loops, BLIP2 visual feedback, and human-in-the-loop validation for iterative quality improvement.

title: CADCodeVerify: Generating CAD Code with Vision-Language Models for 3D Designs
abstract: Introduces CADCodeVerify for iteratively verifying and refining 3D objects created from CAD code using Vision-Language Models. Also presents CADPrompt, the first benchmark for CAD code generation with 200 natural language prompts paired with expert-annotated scripting code. Achieves 7.30% reduction in Point Cloud distance with GPT-4.
method_summary: VLM-based iterative verification and refinement for CAD code generation, plus CADPrompt benchmark (200 prompts with expert annotations) for standardized evaluation.

title: A Multidisciplinary Design and Optimization (MDO) Agent Driven by Large Language Models
abstract: LLM-driven MDO agent that semi-automates end-to-end workflow by orchestrating natural-language-driven parametric modeling, retrieval-augmented generation for knowledge-grounded conceptualization, and intelligent orchestration of engineering software including FEA. Validated across three mechanical design cases.
method_summary: End-to-end LLM agent for multidisciplinary design optimization: NL-to-parametric CAD, RAG for concept generation, and automated FEA orchestration for iterative design verification.

title: Supervising Ralph Wiggum: Exploring a Metacognitive Co-Regulation Agentic AI Loop for Engineering Design
abstract: Addresses design fixation in LLM-based design agents. Proposes Self-Regulation Loop and Co-Regulation Design Agentic Loop where a separate metacognitive agent assists in reducing design fixation. Testing on battery pack design showed co-regulation produces superior designs with comparable computational costs.
method_summary: Metacognitive co-regulation framework for engineering design agents: separate metacognitive agent monitors and corrects design fixation in primary LLM agent, improving design space exploration.

title: From Intent to Execution: Multimodal Chain-of-Thought Reinforcement Learning for Precise CAD Code Generation
abstract: Introduces CAD-RL combining chain-of-thought reasoning with reinforcement learning for converting natural language into executable CAD code. Uses three reward signals (executability, geometric accuracy, external evaluation). Releases ExeCAD dataset of 16,540 real-world CAD examples with paired descriptions.
method_summary: Multimodal CoT-RL framework with triple reward signals for CAD code generation, plus ExeCAD benchmark dataset of 16.5K real-world examples.

title: Toward AI-driven Multimodal Interfaces for Industrial CAD Modeling
abstract: Examines how AI-driven multimodal interfaces can revolutionize industrial 3D CAD modeling. Addresses integration challenges including software constraints and user adoption barriers, analyzing Bayesian workflow inference, multimodal input strategies, and collaborative AI systems for manufacturing.
method_summary: Analysis of multimodal AI interface integration challenges for industrial CAD: Bayesian workflow inference, multi-input strategies, and collaborative AI systems addressing usability in manufacturing contexts.

title: CAD-Llama: Leveraging Large Language Models for Computer-Aided Design Parametric 3D Model Generation
abstract: Transforms parametric 3D CAD commands into Structured Parametric CAD Code (SPCC) with hierarchical semantic descriptions. Employs adaptive pretraining on SPCC followed by instruction tuning to equip LLMs with spatial knowledge. Significantly outperforms prior autoregressive methods and existing LLM baselines.
method_summary: Structured Parametric CAD Code representation with hierarchical semantics, adaptive pretraining and instruction tuning for LLM-based parametric 3D model generation.

title: CADmium: Fine-Tuning Code Language Models for Text-Driven Sequential CAD Design
abstract: System for automating CAD through fine-tuned code language models. Created dataset of 170K+ CAD models with high-quality human-like descriptions generated via GPT-4. Fine-tuned code-LLMs to generate CAD sequences in JSON format from natural language. Introduces geometric and topological evaluation metrics.
method_summary: Code-LLM fine-tuning on 170K GPT-4-annotated CAD models for text-driven sequential design, with novel geometric/topological evaluation metrics. Dataset, code, and models publicly released.

title: PLLM: Pseudo-Labeling Large Language Models for CAD Program Synthesis
abstract: Self-training framework for CAD program recovery from 3D shapes without paired training data. Iteratively samples candidate programs, selects high-fidelity executions, and augments programs to construct synthetic program-shape pairs for fine-tuning. Demonstrates improvements on ABC dataset.
method_summary: Self-training loop for CAD program synthesis: iterative pseudo-labeling of unlabeled 3D shapes via candidate sampling, fidelity selection, and augmentation for LLM fine-tuning.

title: OpenECAD: An Efficient Visual Language Model for Editable 3D-CAD Design
abstract: Series of fine-tuned visual language models (0.55B to 3.1B parameters) that convert images of 3D designs into editable CAD outputs. Generates highly structured 2D sketches and 3D construction commands integrating with existing CAD tools, using specialized datasets from public CAD sources.
method_summary: Lightweight VLMs (0.55B-3.1B) for image-to-editable-CAD conversion with dependency-based sketch generation, designed for integration with existing open-source CAD tools.

title: Automated CAD Modeling Sequence Generation from Text Descriptions via Transformer-Based Large Language Models
abstract: Framework combining LLMs with computer-automated design for industrial CAD generation from text. Includes semi-automated data annotation pipeline using LLMs and VLMs, Transformer-based CAD generator (TCADGen), and CADLLM for sequence refinement using confidence scores.
method_summary: Industrial text-to-CAD pipeline: semi-automated annotation via LLM+VLM, TCADGen for modeling sequence prediction, and CADLLM confidence-based refinement for manufacturing workflows.

title: Text-to-CAD Generation Through Infusing Visual Feedback in Large Language Models
abstract: Introduces CADFusion leveraging LLMs to convert text into CAD parametric sequences. Employs alternating training stages: sequential learning uses ground-truth parametric data, while visual feedback rewards sequences rendering into preferred objects. Demonstrates improved performance qualitatively and quantitatively.
method_summary: Alternating training paradigm (sequential learning + visual feedback reward) for LLM-based text-to-CAD parametric sequence generation with balanced signal learning.

title: Beyond Development: Challenges in Deploying Machine Learning Models for Structural Engineering Applications
abstract: Examines why ML solutions rarely move beyond proof-of-concept in structural engineering despite strong performance. Highlights critical deployment challenges including model overfitting, training data representativeness, variable omission bias, and cross-validation issues. Stresses need for adaptive sampling, physics-informed feature engineering, and balancing model complexity with generalizability.
method_summary: Systematic analysis of ML deployment barriers in structural engineering: overfitting, data representativeness, variable omission, and validation gaps, with recommendations for physics-informed approaches.

title: Naming the Pain in Machine Learning-Enabled Systems Engineering
abstract: International survey of 188 practitioners across 25 countries on engineering ML-enabled systems. Identifies current practices and problems across ML lifecycle phases including deployment and monitoring. Reveals detailed map of practitioner problems and advocates for stronger adoption of software engineering practices in ML system development.
method_summary: Large-scale practitioner survey (188 respondents, 25 countries) mapping ML-enabled systems engineering pain points across lifecycle phases, with deployment and monitoring challenges prominently featured.

title: Towards a Framework for Deep Learning Certification in Safety-Critical Applications Using Inherently Safe Design and Run-Time Error Detection
abstract: Addresses lack of certification processes for deep learning systems in safety-critical domains like aviation. Proposes framework combining inherently safe design with run-time error detection. Evaluates uncertainty quantification, out-of-distribution detection, feature collapse monitoring, and adversarial robustness for safety certification.
method_summary: Certification framework for DL in safety-critical applications: inherently safe design + four run-time safety techniques (UQ, OOD detection, feature collapse monitoring, adversarial robustness).

title: Challenges in Deploying Machine Learning: A Survey of Case Studies
abstract: Survey examining real-world challenges in deploying machine learning models across industries. Covers data management, model training, deployment infrastructure, and monitoring. Identifies recurring patterns of failure including data quality issues, model drift, and integration with existing systems.
method_summary: Comprehensive case-study-based survey of ML deployment challenges across industries, identifying systematic patterns in data management, infrastructure, and monitoring failures.

title: A Survey on AI-Driven Digital Twins in Industry 4.0: Smart Manufacturing and Advanced Robotics
abstract: Reviews over 300 manuscripts on AI-driven digital twin technologies in Industry 4.0. Examines how digital replicas of physical entities integrate with AI across smart manufacturing and robotics, from conventional machining to 3D printing and human-robot collaboration. Identifies practical challenges and proposes integration pathways for multiscale digital twins.
method_summary: Comprehensive survey of 300+ papers on AI-digital twin integration in manufacturing, covering applications from machining to robotics with sustainability analysis across financial, environmental, and social dimensions.

title: AI-Driven Digital Twins for Manufacturing: A Review Across Hierarchical Manufacturing System Levels
abstract: Literature review examining how AI enhances digital twins across four hierarchical manufacturing levels: machine, cell, shop floor, and enterprise. Covers predictive maintenance, process optimization, quality control, and dynamic scheduling using deep RL and CNNs. Identifies challenges in data synchronization, model fidelity, scalability, and security.
method_summary: Hierarchical review of AI-digital twin applications across four manufacturing levels, analyzing DRL/CNN techniques for predictive maintenance, optimization, and quality control with scalability challenges.

title: Artificial Intelligence for Safety-Critical Systems in Industrial and Transportation Domains: A Survey
abstract: Survey on AI for safety-critical systems in industrial and transportation domains. Examines how ML algorithms learn optimized and safe solutions for next-generation autonomous systems. Covers certification challenges, regulatory frameworks, and the gap between AI capabilities and safety requirements in engineering applications.
method_summary: Survey of AI safety-critical system challenges across industrial/transportation domains, analyzing certification gaps, regulatory frameworks, and safety-performance tradeoffs.

title: Zoo.dev Text-to-CAD: Parametric CAD Generation via KCL Programming Language
abstract: Zoo.dev provides a Text-to-CAD API that generates parametric KCL (KittyCAD Language) code from natural language prompts. The platform includes Zoo Design Studio with built-in parametric Text-to-CAD, multi-format export (STEP, STL, OBJ), and pay-as-you-go API access. Represents the first commercial API-first approach to AI-native CAD generation.
method_summary: API-first text-to-CAD platform generating parametric KCL code from natural language, with open-source clients (Python, Rust) and commercial SaaS model for engineering teams.

title: Neural Concept: Physics- and Geometry-Aware AI Design Copilot for Engineering
abstract: Neural Concept provides an AI-first engineering platform combining 3D deep learning with physics-based simulation for product development. The platform enables real-time simulation predictions, design optimization, and a physics-aware AI Design Copilot. Secured $100M Series C funding. Integrates with Siemens NX and NVIDIA Omniverse Blueprint for enterprise-scale deployment.
method_summary: 3D deep learning platform for engineering: physics-informed surrogate models for real-time simulation, geometry-aware design copilot, and enterprise CAD/CAE integration (Siemens, NVIDIA).

title: MecAgent: AI Copilot for Mechanical CAD Software Automation
abstract: MecAgent is an AI copilot for mechanical CAD that generates parametric parts from text descriptions, searches CAD files using keywords or sketches, and provides engineering calculations. Translates natural language intent into automation scripts executable in SolidWorks and other CAD software. Raised $3M seed funding for autonomous mechanical design agents.
method_summary: NL-to-CAD-automation copilot: converts natural language instructions into executable VBA/API scripts for SolidWorks, with parametric part generation and CAD file semantic search.

title: Adam: AI-Native CAD Platform for Text-to-Parametric Design
abstract: Adam is a Y Combinator W25 startup building AI-native CAD that transforms text into parametric designs. Raised $4.1M seed after viral text-to-3D demonstrations reaching 10M impressions. Combines text-to-mesh generation with parametric editing, enabling prompt-based refinement and export of watertight models ready for fabrication. Positions as 'Cursor for CAD'.
method_summary: AI-native CAD platform combining text-to-mesh generation with parametric editing capabilities, targeting 10x faster prototyping for engineering teams via natural language interaction.

title: Leo AI: Large Mechanical Model for CAD-Aware Engineering Assistance
abstract: Leo AI is the first AI platform purpose-built for mechanical engineers, trained on over 1 million engineering sources achieving 96% accuracy. Integrates with PLM and CAD systems (SolidWorks, Onshape), providing engineering calculations, part search, and design recommendations. Raised $9.7M seed backed by Google VP and a16z. Claims to reduce product development timelines by up to 70%.
method_summary: Domain-specific Large Mechanical Model trained on 1M+ engineering sources, integrated with CAD/PLM systems for context-aware engineering assistance with 96% accuracy on mechanical queries.

title: Luminary Cloud: Physics AI Factory for Real-Time Engineering Simulation
abstract: Luminary Cloud provides a GPU-native cloud simulation platform that enables engineers to encode physics into AI models. Integrates with NVIDIA PhysicsNeMo and Omniverse Blueprint for real-time CAE digital twins. Partnership with nTop reduces physics-based AI design optimization from months to hours. Integrates with PTC Onshape for seamless CAD-to-simulation workflows.
method_summary: Cloud-native Physics AI platform: GPU-accelerated CFD/FEA simulation, AI surrogate model training factory, and real-time digital twin visualization via NVIDIA Omniverse integration.

title: Autodesk Neural CAD: 3D Generative AI Foundation Models for Design-to-Make
abstract: Autodesk is developing neural CAD foundation models trained to directly output 3D geometry for design-to-make problems. Building on pioneering generative design in Fusion, the roadmap includes AI-powered manufacturing workflows, Autodesk Assistant (natural language CAD interface), and Intent Driven Design. Represents evolution from generative design to agentic AI with MCP integration.
method_summary: Industry-specific 3D AI foundation models for CAD: neural CAD generation, AI assistant with NL interface, intent-driven design, and MCP protocol integration for agentic workflows.

title: NVIDIA Omniverse Blueprint for Real-Time Computer-Aided Engineering Digital Twins
abstract: NVIDIA Omniverse Blueprint enables industry software developers to build real-time CAE digital twins for aerospace, automotive, and manufacturing. Partners include Altair, Ansys, Cadence, and Siemens. Combines OpenUSD architecture with PhysicsNeMo for physics-informed AI surrogate models, enabling orders-of-magnitude acceleration over traditional simulation.
method_summary: OpenUSD-based platform for real-time physics digital twins: integrates PhysicsNeMo AI surrogates with CAE tools from major vendors, enabling interactive engineering simulation at scale.

title: Siemens NX Design Copilot and Digital Twin Composer for Industrial AI
abstract: Siemens NX introduces Design Copilot (AI-based natural language interface for CAD), immersive engineering with Sony mixed reality, and Digital Twin Composer for industrial metaverse environments. Partnership with NVIDIA for physical AI deployment. Nine new AI copilots across the Xcelerator portfolio for simulation-driven design and manufacturing.
method_summary: AI copilot integration in industrial CAD: NL interface for NX, mixed reality design review, Digital Twin Composer for metaverse-scale simulation, and physical AI deployment with NVIDIA.

title: Dassault Systemes Virtual Companions: AI-Powered Experts on 3DEXPERIENCE Platform
abstract: Dassault introduces Virtual Companions (Aura, Leo, Marie) as AI-powered expert agents on the 3DEXPERIENCE platform. Aura serves as copilot assistant, Marie handles materials/chemistry, Leo focuses on engineering/mechanics/simulation. Live demonstrations showed AI converting 2D sketches into 3D models within seconds, representing shift toward agentic AI in commercial CAD.
method_summary: Multi-agent AI companion system for commercial CAD: domain-specialized agents (copilot, materials, engineering) integrated into 3DEXPERIENCE platform with 2D-to-3D conversion capabilities.

title: Autodesk and Model Context Protocol: Making MCP Enterprise-Ready for CAD
abstract: Autodesk contributed to making the Model Context Protocol enterprise-ready, enabling AI agents to securely connect to CAD tools and data sources. MCP connectors allow Claude and other LLMs to access Autodesk Fusion directly for text-to-CAD generation. Represents the emerging standard for AI-CAD tool interoperability in agentic workflows.
method_summary: Enterprise MCP implementation for CAD: secure AI agent-to-CAD tool connectivity, enabling natural language CAD generation via standardized protocol across multiple AI providers.

title: FreecadMCP: Model Context Protocol Server for AI-Driven CAD Design
abstract: Open-source MCP server connecting FreeCAD to Claude AI and other MCP-ready tools like Cursor. Enables prompt-assisted CAD 3D design through direct AI interaction with FreeCAD, supporting document creation, part insertion, object editing, and parametric operations via the Model Context Protocol standard.
method_summary: Open-source MCP server implementation for FreeCAD: enables LLM agents (Claude, Cursor) to directly control CAD operations through standardized protocol, demonstrating open-source AI-CAD interoperability.
